title: Trump’s Portrayal of the War in Iran Collides With Reality
summary: President Trump is confronting a crisis that is not bending to his narrative of a “pretty reasonable” new regime in Iran and all-but-assured victory for the United States.

title: What the Iran War Means for China
summary: Our national security correspondent David E. Sanger examines what the Iran war means to China, which is the world’s biggest importer of Iranian oil.

title: Senate Republicans Again Block Bid to Limit Trump’s Iran War Powers
summary: For the fourth time since the war began, G.O.P. senators successfully fended off an effort to constrain the president. But there were signs of growing unease among Republicans.

title: With Iran War on Pause, Political Battle Resumes Over Israeli Democracy
summary: A court fight over Itamar Ben-Gvir, an extremist who oversees the police, pits the attorney general against Benjamin Netanyahu and his right-wing government.

title: Stocks Hit a Record High
summary: Also, Pakistani mediators arrive in Iran. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.

title: Trump Breaks With Meloni, Italy’s Leader, Amid Dispute Over Pope and Iran
summary: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni was once considered one of President Trump’s closest European allies. Their friendship now appears in danger.

title: Just War Doctrine: The Pope, JD Vance and a Theological Debate
summary: President Trump’s attack on Pope Leo has touched off an argument about a framework for determining when war is justified.

title: In the House, Republican Plans Go Awry Amid Party Divides
summary: Fresh off a two-week break, lawmakers returned to turmoil in the House, where legislation to reopen the Department of Homeland Security is stalled and the G.O.P. is struggling to keep its agenda on track.

title: Labor Department Investigates Texts Sent Among Staff, Secretary and Her Family
summary: Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer asked staff members to bring wine to her hotel room, and to keep in touch with her husband and father.

title: Trump’s Quest for More Sway Over Fed Faces Fresh Hurdles
summary: Jerome H. Powell’s term as chair of the Federal Reserve ends on May 15, but it appears increasingly likely that he will stay on in some capacity at the central bank after that.

title: DeSantis Delays Redistricting Special Session and Adds a Vaccine Bill
summary: The Florida governor is expected to propose a congressional map that could result in up to five new Republican-leaning seats. Some in the party fear it could backfire.

title: S&P 500 Hits Record High as Stock Market Looks Beyond Iran War
summary: Investors appear to be treating an end to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran as a foregone conclusion, as the S&P 500 closes above 7,000.

title: Debt Alarms Ring as Countries Rack Up More Emergency Spending
summary: Amid signs of a prolonged period of high energy costs, policymakers are urging restraint as governments open up the public till to protect households and businesses.

title: That Meeting You Hate May Keep A.I. From Stealing Your Job
summary: As artificial intelligence makes many tasks easier, the human work of cajoling, arm-twisting and reassuring appears to be rising in importance.

title: What Is ‘Jagged Intelligence’ and How Can It Reframe the AI Debate?
summary: A.I. has always been compared to human intelligence, but that may not be the right way to think about it. What it does well can help predict what jobs it may replace.

title: Sister Mary Kay and the Waning Days of the Sisters of Charity
summary: An influential order of nuns decided to complete its mission when the last sister dies. The only question left is how to finish well.

title: Trump’s ‘Triumphal Arch’ Draws Backlash, Even From an Expert Who Proposed It
summary: The story of how President Trump quadrupled the size of the original proposal for the arch follows a now-familiar pattern.

title: Saudi Fund to Back Away From LIV Golf Under Mounting Financial Pressures
summary: The Saudi league, established in 2022, attracted some of the sport’s biggest stars with huge contracts.

title: Yale Report Finds Colleges Deserve Blame for Higher Education’s Problems
summary: A 10-member committee offered a brutal assessment of academia’s role in creating the forces challenging American colleges and universities.

title: Live Nation and Ticketmaster Illegally Monopolized Ticketing Market, Jury Finds
summary: In a verdict that could have far-reaching consequences in the music industry, the live colossus that includes Ticketmaster was found to have violated antitrust laws.

title: 5 Takeaways From the Live Nation Antitrust Trial
summary: A jury found that the concert giant operated as a monopoly, a verdict that could have major reverberations in the music industry.

title: The Pied-à-Terre Tax Has Failed Before. Could This Year Be Different?
summary: Amid calls from the left to tax the rich, a tax proposal on multimillion-dollar second homes in New York City, backed by Gov. Kathy Hochul, seems to have better odds of passing than in years past.

title: What Is the Pied-à-Terre Tax? 5 Things to Know About Hochul’s Proposal.
summary: Gov. Kathy Hochul wants to tax second homes in New York City that are worth $5 million or more. Here’s how the proposal might work.

title: Analysis of Alzheimer’s Drugs Stirs Debate About Their Effectiveness
summary: The review said a certain class of drugs had little clinical benefit, but many Alzheimer’s experts criticized the analysis, saying it unfairly lumped failed drugs with two recently approved treatments.

title: He Preached the Gospel on the Subway. Then He Pulled Out a Machete.
summary: Anthony Griffin was a popular battle rapper who turned toward religious rhetoric. The police say he slashed three people with a machete before they killed him.

title: How The Times Covers Attackers, Suspects and Victims of Violence
summary: Reporting on the people who upend life and those whose lives are upended can bring surprising and uncomfortable details to light.

title: In Defense of Dumb Dogs
summary: Your pet is (probably) not a genius, and that’s OK.

title: After Mythos, the Future of the Internet Is At a Crossroads
summary: In the race to patch up cybersecurity holes found by the newest A.I. models, we risk leaving too many people to fend for themselves.

title: Sudan Enters Fourth Year of War Amid World’s Most Severe Humanitarian Crisis
summary: Fighting that erupted in 2023 has forced millions from their homes and left widespread hunger that is now being compounded by strife in the Middle East.

title: Justice Sotomayor Apologizes for Highly Personal Criticism of Justice Kavanaugh
summary: At the University of Kansas School of Law last week, she criticized her colleague while discussing his views in an immigration-related case.

title: Mamdani’s Wife Admits ‘Shame’ Over Social Media Posts From Her Teens
summary: Rama Duwaji apologized for using what she said was “harmful” language as a teenager, in her first interview since her husband, Zohran Mamdani, took office.

title: Videos Show Officers Beating Man During Arrest in Brooklyn
summary: Two officers were placed under investigation after the videos surfaced. Mayor Zohran Mamdani called them disturbing. The Police Department said officers incorrectly identified the man.
